creator: jdc@koitsu.org
text:
A very large number of files in the ports tree contain trailing spaces
	at the end of their lines, including lines that look blank but actually
	contain nothing more than the equivalent of " \n".  Some lines even
	contain multiple trailing spaces.

	It would be good to clean all of these up, since some are on lines that
	may anger future parsing efforts or who knows what else.

	I explicitly excluded pkg-descr and pkg-message from the list of files to
	examine, since lots of ports have trailing spaces in there.  Someone should
	certainly clean these up too, but, well, baby steps...  :-)

	Other stuff that needs to get excluded are things under Templates/Licenses,
	since retaining the originals there is ideal.  For example the PHP301 license
	has trailing spaces -- but does the ACTUAL ORIGINAL from the authors' site
	or was this the result of someone's botched copy-paste or editor snafu?

Fix: 

Automatically removing all trailing spaces can be done with perl -pi -e,
	but one needs to be very careful what files this gets applied to, because
	modifying patches in files/ would be bad -- but on the other hand, rc.d
	startup scripts (which live in files/ usually) should be analysed.  But
	like I said, baby steps.

	To make things easier on folks, I can provide a patch that would clean
	most of this up, but it's going to be a big diff.  Let me know.
How-To-Repeat: 	find /usr/ports -maxdepth 3 -not \( -name "pkg-descr" -o -name "pkg-message" \) -exec egrep -H -n ' $' {} \;
